title: Primary, secondary and tertiary education enrollment and completion rates
subtitle: This is given as the gross rate, which includes children of any age entering the level of education; this can result in percentages greater than 100 because children may enter education late or repeat a year.
caption:
Primary, secondary and tertiary education enrollment and completion rates, World

This is given as the gross rate, which includes children of any age entering the level of education; this can result in percentages greater than 100 because children may enter education late or repeat a year.

Across the globe from 1970 to 2024, five distinct lines track the gross enrollment and completion rates for different levels of education, measured in percentages on the vertical axis. The blue line representing primary enrollment occupies the highest position throughout the timeline, starting near 90% in 1970, rising to a peak above 100% around 2008, and fluctuating between 100% and 105% through 2024. Below this, the green line for primary completion begins around 1999 near 78% and climbs steadily to approximately 88% by the end of the period. The orange line for secondary enrollment and the purple line for lower secondary completion follow similar upward trajectories; secondary enrollment rises from roughly 40% in 1970 to nearly 80% in 2024, while lower secondary completion starts near 60% in 2000 and converges with secondary enrollment near the 78% mark by 2024. The brown line indicating tertiary enrollment remains the lowest curve, beginning at roughly 10% in 1970 and accelerating its ascent after 2000 to reach just above 40% in 2024. The data shows a consistent expansion of participation across all education levels over the fifty-four year span, with primary enrollment consistently exceeding the 100% threshold in the twenty-first century while tertiary enrollment remains the only category below 50%.
